title: 💻 Copilot Code Impact [v2]
description: Measures the volume and quality of Copilot-assisted code contributions including lines suggested, accepted, acceptance rate, and PR attribution.

panels.title: Data Source
panels.description: Shows the current data source mode (live, seed, or demo) and last sync status.

options.content:
## 📊 Custom Metrics Dashboard v2
Data mode and sync status shown below.

panels.title: Lines Accepted (28d)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Total lines of code accepted from Copilot suggestions over the last 28 days (or selected time range).

### What insight it offers
Absolute measure of AI-assisted code production. Growing numbers indicate increasing Copilot utilization across the team.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Derived from loc_added_sum in copilot_org_metrics
- Does not distinguish between languages or quality of accepted code

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(loc_added_sum), 0) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.title: Lines Suggested (28d)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Total lines of code suggested by Copilot over the last 28 days (or selected time range).

### What insight it offers
High suggestion volume relative to accepted lines reveals developer trust levels. Compare with Lines Accepted to gauge effectiveness.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Derived from loc_suggested_to_add_sum in copilot_org_metrics
- Includes all suggestions regardless of whether they were viewed

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) AS "Lines Suggested (28d)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.title: Acceptance Rate (%)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Percentage of Copilot-suggested lines that were accepted by developers in the selected time range.

### What insight it offers
Higher acceptance rates indicate more relevant and accurate suggestions. Industry benchmarks typically range from 25% to 45%.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Computed as loc_added_sum / loc_suggested_to_add_sum across all days in range
- Language mix and developer experience level affect acceptance rates

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(ROUND(
  SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1
), 0) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date

panels.title: Copilot-Attributed PRs
panels.description:
### What it measures
Count of merged PRs authored by developers with an active Copilot seat in the selected time range.

### What insight it offers
Shows the reach of Copilot across the team's output. Useful for understanding how much of your codebase may have AI-assisted contributions.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- A seat holder may not use Copilot for every PR
- Only counts merged PRs

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id) AS "Copilot-Attributed PRs"
FROM pull_requests pr
JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.title: Lines Accepted vs Suggested over Time
panels.description:
### What it measures
Daily trend of lines suggested by Copilot versus lines accepted by developers.

### What insight it offers
The gap between suggested and accepted lines shows how much assistance is offered versus taken. A narrowing gap indicates improving suggestion quality or developer trust.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Daily granularity from copilot_org_metrics
- Weekend dips are expected due to reduced developer activity

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  loc_suggested_to_add_sum AS "Lines Suggested",
  loc_added_sum AS "Lines Added"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

defaults.noValue: No Copilot metrics — org may not have a Copilot license

panels.title: Acceptance Rate Trend
panels.description:
### What it measures
Daily Copilot suggestion acceptance rate as a percentage over time.

### What insight it offers
An upward trend may indicate improving model quality, better developer familiarity with Copilot, or a shift toward tasks where Copilot excels.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Daily rate can be noisy; use a moving average mentally when interpreting trends
- Low suggestion volume days may produce extreme rates

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  day AS time,
  ROUND(loc_added_sum::numeric / NULLIF(loc_suggested_to_add_sum, 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
ORDER BY day

defaults.noValue: No Copilot metrics — org may not have a Copilot license

panels.title: Copilot PR Rate (%)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Percentage of all merged PRs in the time range that were authored by active Copilot seat holders.

### What insight it offers
Tracks the proportion of team output with potential AI assistance. Growing rate indicates expanding Copilot adoption impact.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Copilot seat holder is not the same as Copilot was used for the PR
- Only counts merged PRs

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT COALESCE(ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
), 0) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

panels.title: Top 10 by Lines Added (Copilot Users)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Top 10 developers ranked by total lines accepted from Copilot suggestions in the selected time range.

### What insight it offers
Identifies power users and Copilot champions. These developers can serve as internal advocates and trainers for the broader team.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Only includes developers with copilot_user_metrics records
- High lines added does not necessarily mean high code quality

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  user_login AS "Developer",
  SUM(loc_added_sum) AS "Lines Added",
  ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_user_metrics
WHERE day BETWEEN $__timeFrom()::date AND $__timeTo()::date
GROUP BY user_login
ORDER BY "Lines Added" DESC
LIMIT 10

defaults.noValue: No Copilot user metrics — org may not have a Copilot license
